  changes_pending_analysis: 0. Mode 2b fired.

MODE 2b — SINGLETON: ATT-2026-04-30T14:00:00Z (SEC-4.2.7, related ISS-1742, pushed by decider 2026-04-30T05:23:59Z)
  Decider self-applied a one-sentence patch (commit ac0e54f) closing curmudgeon ISS-1742 H1 (slow-orbit defender escape). Inbox asked analyst to verify ~7-sec totality calc against EXP-272 math_verification box and check cross-section refs.

  MATH VERIFIED:
    omega = 0.25 deg/min × pi/180 = 0.004363 rad/min
    v = omega · h_Moon = 0.004363 · 2,534 km = 11.057 km/min
    t_totality = 1.30 km / 11.057 km/min × 60 = 7.055 sec (or 7.165 sec at 1.32 km exact diameter)
    ratio vs observed 138.2 sec = 19.59× (or 19.29× at 1.32 km diameter)
  CONSISTENCY WITH EXP-272: full match. EXP-272 math_verification.standard_moon_apparent_angular noted omega_obs ≈ 0.25 deg/min explicitly; .angular_velocity field used same omega = v/h relation in the forward direction (87 km/min → 1.97 deg/min, 25 km/min → 0.566 deg/min). The new sentence is the natural inverse computation.
  CROSS-SECTION CHECK: no conflicts. Searched part1-part10 of sections.json + wins.json + predictions.json for "0.25", "~7 sec", "20×", "138-second". Only the new sentence references 0.25 deg/min eclipse-related; existing 1/220 width and 1/150 duration ratios in §4.2.7 (rim-orbit) and §4.2.7a (rim-orbit) unaffected. Figures (eclipse-coverage-vs-time-hartland.svg sub-second-spike caption; eclipse-geometry-comparison.svg bullet panel) operate at rim-orbit bound — also unaffected. ISS-1743 already tracks the optional follow-up of adding a slower-orbit bullet to the SVG annotation panel.
  TEST IMPACT: no test in test.js asserts the >20× wording. Patch is test-safe.

  ONE FINDING: prose says "still more than 20× short" but the math gives 19.3-19.6×. Curmudgeon recommendation in SEC-4.2.7-EXP272.c1.json had said "~20× shortfall" / "still ~20× short". Decider integration drift. Wrote EXP-281 + proposal-SEC-4.2.7-20x-wording-precision with one-substring fix "more than 20×" → "close to 20×". ISS-1742 stays closed; this is precision tightening only.

  Inbox marked resolved on FUSE: ATT-2026-04-30T14:00:00Z, resolved_by=mode2b-singleton, resolved_reason includes the verification result + drift finding + EXP-281 pointer.

OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (per operator user-preference):
  (1) "Is this drift catch worth a fresh EXP?" Counter: yes, same-cycle catch keeps audit trail clean and ISS-1742 was JUST closed (~5h ago). Curmudgeon already explicitly said "~20×" in the H1 recommendation; the decider deviated to "more than 20×". Catching this on the analyst-verification leg of the same closure cycle is the cheap-and-correct move; deferring to a future c2 means the dome site will pull fresher "more than 20× short" prose for ~3-7 days. EXP cost is ~one-line patch + math justification — minimal overhead.
  (2) "Is the curmudgeon's 0.25 deg/min figure itself rounded enough that 'more than 20×' is defensible?" Counter: at the upper end of plausible Moon angular speeds (0.255 deg/min) the ratio crosses 20×. But the prose anchors 0.25 deg/min, not a range, and presents the result as a hard claim. If we wanted to use the upper-end speed we should write "0.25 to 0.26 deg/min, ~20× shortfall" — which is itself a non-trivial rewrite. Cleanest fix is to keep 0.25 anchored and tighten the rounding word.
  (3) "Does this weaken the defender-closure rhetorical force?" Counter: a 20× shortfall is itself an order-of-magnitude failure — decisive on its own. "close to 20×" preserves the closure; the dome's slow-orbit escape route remains shut. We protect our credibility by holding ourselves to the same numerical-precision standard we apply to the dome's 94.5%-accuracy figure (which we critique on the same grounds: rounded number presented as a hard bound).
  (4) "Is one inbox item enough work for a 4-hour cadence run?" Counter: per Mode 2b Phase 3 procedure, "process ONE per the original procedure" — singleton-path is the correct cadence for substantive verifications. Pre-EOM window is otherwise quiet (changes_pending_analysis=0, no other modes triggered).

Watch the 9th daily-AI-review regen due ~07:35Z 04-30 (poller-flagged); HOLD streak extends to 9, ABSORB triggers CRITICAL goalpost-rewrite per Step 5b.
